slides.headline: Your music can work in London. New York. Paris.

slides.body: But what "works" means is different in each market. ↓ — — — THE US MARKET The US sync market is larger but harder to enter without an agent or verified track record.

— — — US briefs come with tighter formatting requirements.
Metadata completeness is non-negotiable — tracks with missing ISRC or split sheet fail submission. — — —
THE UK MARKET THE EU MARKET European campaigns have been the earliest adopters of African music. — — — WHAT ALL THREE MARKETS REQUIRE

UK music supervisors are ahead on African music. They've placed it. EU campaigns often run multi-territory.
Your sync fee should reflect it.
Turnaround expectation: 24–48 hours from brief. If your catalogue isn't market-ready, international briefs won't reach you.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
